description: I was organizing photos and came across the ones submitted with this report that appear to show a male Mexican Duck with a male and female Mallard. These photos were taken during the Pueblo Reservoir CBC prior to Mexican Duck being elevated to species status in 2018.  This description is based entirely on the photos.  Dark-bodied duck similar in size and structure as the accompanying Mallards - with general plumage characteristics more similar to the female than the male. The head and neck were pale and sharply contrasting with the dark body.  The bill was greenish-yellow.  Uppertail coverts lacked any indication of a curl.
photographer: Photographer: Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner
